source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Figure $\mathrm{S}$ is made from four paper ribbons $10 \mathrm{~cm}$ wide. Each of the ribbons is $25 \mathrm{~cm}$ longer than the previous one (see the picture). By how many centimetres will the perimeter of figure $\mathrm{T}$ (made from the same ribbons) exceed that of figure $\mathrm{S}$?
<image1>

image_dependency: the task compares perimeters of figures S and T formed by ribbons, so the arrangement/shape in the image is central
multi_step: the problem involves four ribbons with successive lengths differing by 25 cm
it asks for the perimeter difference between two different figures made from the same ribbons, implying combination of ribbon-length information with visual arrangement
the answer is not directly read off; it likely requires at least interpreting the figure and then computing/comparing perimeters
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is '50'
registry_passed=true with image and text assets present
a numeric perimeter-difference result is amenable to rule-based or solver-based checking once the figure interpretation is fixed

risk_flags: geometry/layout details are contained in the image rather than fully specified in text

decision_reasons: question, answer, and primary image are all present with no registry issues
the sample is highly image-dependent, but the required image asset exists, so the core geometry/layout semantics are recoverable
verifiability is high because the target answer is numeric and checkable
overall scoring is strong with good multi-step potential and no evidence of missing core assets
